kept_findings.notes: Baseline: group=32 tile=64 beta=0.267 ndsp=32 pte=256
Increase group size to 64: more WHT mixing -> better compression, larger errors
Group size 128: maximum compression ratio, expect error degradation
Lower beta* divisor -> easier to be Q4 -> more Q4 -> more compression, higher er
Double DSP count to 64: 2x compute throughput, check if still compute-bound
128 DSPs: 4x throughput, roofline may shift to memory-bound for typical workload

design_space_conclusions: Group size sensitivity: best group_size=128 (FOM=1.9200). Overhead per element decreases with larger group but error grows as WHT mixes more elements.
Beta* sensitivity: best divisor=0.15 (FOM=1.9557). Controls Q4/Q8 split; conservative (higher divisor) favours quality, aggressive (lower) favours compression.
Tile size: best tile_size=128 (FOM=1.6865). Larger tiles reduce softmax rescale overhead; smaller tiles have lower on-chip footprint.
DSP count: best n_dsp=128 (FOM=3.6412). At n_dsp=64, LACU becomes memory-bound — extra DSPs are wasted.
Codebook: uniform vs Lloyd-Max. Uniform FOM=1.9827 (norm_err=0.05204). Lloyd-Max provides better error bounds; uniform is simpler RTL but measurably worse.
